scope: BTC, ETH, and SOL Polymarket five-minute paper research

implementation_limitations.limitation: The 39 frozen predictors do not directly include the chosen outcome's absolute executable bid/ask, active tick, minimum order size, dynamic fee rate/exponent, or recorded venue taker delay.
implementation_limitations.effect: Exact depth, fee, tick, size, and latency still determine every label, but the baseline can only infer parts of that execution state indirectly from relative book, age, depth, liquidity, and volume features.
implementation_limitations.handling: Do not retrofit Round 9 after observing results. If it fails, preregister these decision-time execution fields in a future independent challenger.

implementation_limitations.limitation: The positive_complete classifier is a composite hurdle: entry availability, FOK fill, exit fill, and strictly positive after-cost round-trip utility must all succeed.
implementation_limitations.effect: A single ridge or MLP probability cannot distinguish fill failure from a completed but unprofitable action, which can limit calibration and economic ranking under class imbalance.
implementation_limitations.handling: Keep the frozen baseline intact. A future challenger may separately model fill probability and conditional utility or rank actions by a calibrated utility distribution, with new untouched evidence.

implementation_limitations.limitation: A missing post-submission entry execution book or an entry receipt that first appears inside the excluded close window leaves the hypothetical order state unproven.
implementation_limitations.effect: Treating either state as a known no-fill or silently excluding it would invent execution evidence or condition model admission on future observability.
implementation_limitations.handling: Keep the diagnostic action-unavailable label, but fail the entire ridge admission when either terminal count is nonzero. A definite tick-invalid server rejection remains a classifier-eligible zero-utility no-fill. This safeguard does not change the frozen features, split, thresholds, latency, costs, or utility and grants no profitability or trading claim.

findings.title: Polymarket CLOB V2 Migration and Dynamic Fees
findings.finding: CLOB V2 has been the production protocol since 2026-04-28. The production host remains clob.polymarket.com, fees are dynamic per market and applied at match time, and legacy V1-signed orders have no production compatibility. Official V2 SDK revision fdb2590dc85e600ad98f1f668ea62a0627554d73 caches fd.r/fd.e for platform-fee balance adjustment, omits feeRateBps from V2 signed orders, and resolves builder fees to zero when no builder code is attached.
findings.round_9_application: The paper replay queries and hash-binds V2 market info, cross-checks Gamma fee schedules, charges the recorded fd curve on both taker legs, and grants no order authority or builder attribution. Recorded mbf, tbf, and token base_fee values remain provenance and are not added to the V2 dynamic curve; doing so would double-charge a V1/base-fee field that the official V2 SDK does not add. Any future Polymarket order client must use V2 semantics and pass a separate live-readiness review.

findings.title: Polymarket Crypto Taker Delay Semantics
findings.finding: Polymarket's official 2026-06-05 CLOB maintenance record identifies a fixed 250 ms crypto taker delay. Current CLOB V2 schemas expose its enable flag as itode independently from the general market delay exposed as compact sd or full seconds_delay; itode=true and seconds_delay=0 are therefore not contradictory.
findings.round_9_application: Replay maps the hash-bound itode flag to exactly 250 ms for each marketable leg and separately parses sd from the same immutable CLOB payload. A nonzero general delay fails closed because no authoritative composition rule with the crypto delay has been established. The six current/next BTC, ETH, and SOL markets sampled at 2026-07-15T23:41Z all exposed itode=true, omitted sd, and returned full seconds_delay=0; that bounded live check validates plumbing but is not treated as model evidence.

findings.title: Deep Limit Order Book Forecasting
findings.finding: High predictive accuracy need not produce actionable trades; evaluation should measure complete executable transactions.
findings.round_9_application: The frozen label is a causal two-leg FOK round trip after recorded depth, fees, and latency, not a direction-only accuracy target.

findings.title: Forecasting High Frequency Order Flow Imbalance
findings.finding: Event-time order arrivals and their clustering can contain information lost by fixed-window aggregation.
findings.round_9_application: Round 9 retains its frozen 100/250/1000/5000 ms causal Binance imbalance inputs. An event-time Hawkes challenger may be preregistered only after the ridge result; it cannot be retrofitted into this confirmation.

findings.title: Explainable Patterns in Cryptocurrency Microstructure
findings.finding: Order-flow imbalance, spread, depth, and adverse-selection features can transfer across crypto assets, but tradability must be checked after conservative execution costs.
findings.round_9_application: The frozen vector already contains causal imbalance, spread, depth, volatility, and age features. CatBoost or GMADL remains an untested future challenger and has no presumed edge.

findings.title: The Anatomy of a Decentralized Prediction Market: Microstructure Evidence from the Polymarket Order Book
findings.finding: Polymarket public-feed trade-direction inference agrees with on-chain direction only about 59 percent of the time; change-side data is not an aggressor label.
findings.round_9_application: No Polymarket aggressor-direction feature is used. Public change-side values reconstruct bid and ask books only; directional imbalance comes from Binance's explicit aggressor flag.

findings.title: Settlement Manipulation in Prediction Markets
findings.finding: Five-minute Bitcoin contracts exhibit abnormal underlying order flow and reversals in the final settlement seconds.
findings.round_9_application: The frozen execution path rejects entries and exits inside the final 30 seconds. That exclusion remains mandatory and cannot be relaxed to increase trade count.

findings.title: PMXT Polymarket Orderbook Archive v2
findings.finding: The public archive stores native Polymarket market-channel book, price-change, last-trade-price, and tick-size-change events in hourly ZSTD Parquet from 2026-04-13T19Z, including source and collector-receipt timestamps.
findings.round_9_application: This is the preferred historical tick-source candidate after the prospective baseline. It is not Round 9 evidence: every selected hourly object still needs a local SHA-256 inventory, BTC/ETH/SOL market mapping, gap certificate, snapshot-consistency replay, official outcome join, and exact fee/execution-metadata join. Its side field is never an aggressor label.

findings.title: Polymarket 5-Minute Crypto Up/Down Markets
findings.finding: The CC0 release contains 26,858,579 one-second top-of-book samples across about 89,000 markets from March to May 2026. Outcomes are inferred, ask depth beyond the touch is absent, and there are no underlying-price, local receipt-clock, fee, or execution-parameter records.
findings.round_9_application: It may support bounded market-state or data-shape development only after the six BTC/ETH/SOL Parquet files match the preregistered sizes and LFS SHA-256 values below and outcomes are independently resolved. It cannot support fill, latency, after-cost PnL, confirmation, or trading-authority claims.

findings.title: Polymarket Crypto 5-Minute and 15-Minute Market Data
findings.finding: The third-party dataset reports ten-second order books and one-minute Binance candles, without the recorder's local receipt clocks or exact execution-parameter snapshots.
findings.round_9_application: It is insufficient for subsecond causal fill labels and is excluded from Round 9 confirmation evidence. Any later use requires an independent provenance and integrity audit.
